quizQuestion:
{___} más despacio, por favor.
(Speak more slowly, please.)
explanation: habla — tú affirmative imperative of hablar. Regular -ar: same as él/ella present tense. The tú imperative is used for direct commands to one person (informal).

quizQuestion:
{___} las verduras — son buenas para la salud.
(Eat your vegetables — they're good for your health.)
sentenceTranslation: Eat your vegetables — they're good for your health.
explanation: come — tú affirmative imperative of comer. Regular -er: same as él/ella present tense.

quizQuestion:
{___} tu nombre y apellido aquí.
(Write your first and last name here.)
sentenceTranslation: Write your first and last name here.
explanation: escribe — tú affirmative imperative of escribir. Regular -ir: same as él/ella present tense.
ven — irregular tú imperative of venir. The 8 irregular tú imperatives are: ven, ten, pon, sal, di, haz, ve, sé. All are monosyllabic.

quizQuestion:
{___} cuidado con el perro.
(Be careful with the dog.)
explanation: ten — irregular tú imperative of tener. 'Ten cuidado' (be careful) is one of the most common imperative phrases in Spanish.

quizQuestion:
{___} el libro en la mesa, por favor.
(Put the book on the table, please.)
sentenceTranslation: Put the book on the table, please.
explanation: pon — irregular tú imperative of poner.

quizQuestion:
{___} de aquí ahora mismo.
(Get out of here right now.)
sentenceTranslation: Get out of here right now.
explanation: sal — irregular tú imperative of salir.

quizQuestion:
{___} la verdad — ¿qué pasó realmente?
(Tell the truth — what really happened?)
sentenceTranslation: Tell the truth — what really happened?
explanation: di — irregular tú imperative of decir. One of the 8 irregular tú imperatives. 'Di la verdad' (tell the truth) is very common.

quizQuestion:
{___} los deberes antes de salir.
(Do your homework before going out.)
sentenceTranslation: Do your homework before going out.
explanation: haz — irregular tú imperative of hacer. One of the 8 irregular tú imperatives.

quizQuestion:
{___} al mercado y compra pan.
(Go to the market and buy some bread.)
sentenceTranslation: Go to the market and buy some bread.
explanation: ve — irregular tú imperative of ir. One of the 8 irregular tú imperatives. Note: 've' (from ir) is different from 'ves' (you see, from ver).

quizQuestion:
{___} amable con todo el mundo.
(Be kind to everyone.)
explanation: sé — irregular tú imperative of ser. Note the written accent to distinguish from 'se' (reflexive pronoun) and 'sé' (I know, from saber).

quizQuestion:
En este país {___} español.
(In this country Spanish is spoken.)
sentenceTranslation: In this country Spanish is spoken.
grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se habla (Spanish is spoken)
explanation: se habla — impersonal se construction. Structure: se + third person singular verb. Expresses passive/impersonal actions without mentioning the agent.

quizQuestion:
En este cartel dice: '{___} piso'.
(This sign says: 'Apartment for sale'.)
grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se vende (for sale — singular)
explanation: se vende — impersonal se. Singular noun (piso) → third person singular verb (vende). For plural nouns: 'Se venden coches' (Cars for sale).

quizQuestion:
En este restaurante {___} muy bien.
(In this restaurant you eat very well.)
sentenceTranslation: In this restaurant you eat very well.
grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se come (one eats / you eat)
explanation: se come — impersonal se with intransitive sense. Expresses a general truth without a specific subject. Common in restaurant and travel contexts.
